response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced senior web and full-stack engineer with a broad background spanning TypeScript, Node.js, and some embedded exposure, but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The most disqualifying factor is the complete absence of Japanese language proficiency, which is a hard requirement. Beyond that, his career has been oriented toward web platforms, data pipelines, and developer tooling — not embedded firmware, LAN-switch networking, or security protocols like MACsec or 802.1X. While he lists C in his skills and has brief C/C++ exposure at National Instruments, there is no evidence of Linux kernel-level development or low-level networking stack work required by this position.
strengths: 16+ years of software development experience demonstrates solid engineering fundamentals and breadth across multiple domains
Has touched embedded systems and hardware control (robotic arms at Altom, Sound Position Server with Linux/C++), indicating some low-level exposure
Strong Linux background and experience with performance-sensitive systems (low-latency apps at Sociomantic) shows systems-level awareness
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — this is a hard, non-negotiable requirement for the role
Zero experience with security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, encryption, authentication systems) or LAN-switch/Layer-2/Layer-3 networking
Primary technical stack is TypeScript/Node.js/web — C programming is peripheral and there is no evidence of Linux kernel source-level investigation or modification

key_factors.factor: No security or embedded networking experience
Broad engineering experience and Linux familiarity
